title: No EVA news at NYC Anime Festival
display_title: No EVA news at NYC Anime Festival
wikitext:
[]: No EVA news at NYC Anime Festival             Quote Post by FinalImpactCrisis » Sun Dec 09, 2007 9:02 pm Hey all, I just got back from the NY Anime Festival (which was kind of dead overall, I don't know what this says about the current American anime market) and there wasn't much Evangelion presence at all. In fact, judging by this convention, current anime fans are only into Naruto, Bleach, Deathnote, and video games. Everything else was kind of toy and hentai-based. Not a whole lot of fun. In fact, if I were just getting into anime, I would have no idea that Evangelion existed at all. I hate to say this, but it looks like Eva missed a generation between Pokemon and Yu-gi-Oh. In any case, I did pick up some cool t-shirts, dvds, and free posters. FIC

[]: Re: No EVA news at NYC Anime Festival             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Dec 09, 2007 9:22 pm FinalImpactCrisis wrote: In fact, judging by this convention, current anime fans are only into Naruto, Bleach, Deathnote, and video games. In fact, if I were just getting into anime, I would have no idea that Evangelion existed at all.

[]: Re: No EVA news at NYC Anime Festival             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 09, 2007 9:46 pm BobBQ wrote: Whither the good old days?

[]: Re: No EVA news at NYC Anime Festival             Quote Post by V » Sun Dec 09, 2007 9:49 pm Reichu wrote: BobBQ wrote: Whither the good old days?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Dec 09, 2007 9:57 pm I'll believe when I see it, and not a moment before.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 09, 2007 9:59 pm If I got $5 every time V started going on about creating the greatest NGE site in the universe......

[]: Re: No EVA news at NYC Anime Festival             Quote Post by drinian » Sun Dec 09, 2007 10:00 pm Thanks for the report. FinalImpactCrisis wrote: kind of dead overall, I don't know what this says about the current American anime market)

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Mon Dec 10, 2007 1:49 am Reichu wrote: If I got $5 every time V started going on about creating the greatest NGE site in the universe......

[]: Re: No EVA news at NYC Anime Festival             Quote Post by EvaCub » Mon Dec 10, 2007 9:25 am FinalImpactCrisis wrote: I hate to say this, but it looks like Eva missed a generation between Pokemon and Yu-gi-Oh.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Dec 10, 2007 9:47 am Reichu wrote: If I got $5 every time V started going on about creating the greatest NGE site in the universe......

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Mon Dec 10, 2007 11:31 am Reichu wrote: If I got $5 every time V started going on about creating the greatest NGE site in the universe......

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Dec 10, 2007 3:59 pm @ V: Don't forget about Step 6! If all else fails, we take the money and run away to Rio! :) (If you don't get the slightly changed reference, you suck.) Reichu wrote: They went bye-bye when nth-generation VHS bootlegs were replaced by BitTorrent. I KNOW -- I WAS THERE.

[]:             Quote Post by FinalImpactCrisis » Mon Dec 10, 2007 6:03 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: @ V: Don't forget about Step 6! If all else fails, we take the money and run away to Rio! :) (If you don't get the slightly changed reference, you suck.) Reichu wrote: They went bye-bye when nth-generation VHS bootlegs were replaced by BitTorrent. I KNOW -- I WAS THERE.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Dec 10, 2007 6:43 pm There was a lot of Evangelion presence at Fanime. There were weirdou toys and everything, and Yamaga-san handed out Weva posters to people who came to Friday's GAINAX panel.

[]: Re: No EVA news at NYC Anime Festival             Quote Post by Matt_Colly » Mon Dec 10, 2007 7:52 pm BobBQ wrote: FinalImpactCrisis wrote: In fact, judging by this convention, current anime fans are only into Naruto, Bleach, Deathnote, and video games. In fact, if I were just getting into anime, I would have no idea that Evangelion existed at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Mon Dec 10, 2007 8:01 pm Hell yes. When Rebuild gets licensed, there'll be HUGE amounts of fans at cons, and ELAM will just make it bigger. Just wait, when ELAM comes out (whenever that is) we get to say, "We knew about this first. And this sucks compared to THIS." *stuff a Platinum thinpak in their hands* And Rebuild is going to be awesome. I can feel it. This is Anno's biggest project since, well, EoE, and he's already doing amazing. Webuild may end up being just as good or better than what we already have. And that will get people excited. Really excited.

[]:             Quote Post by Matt_Colly » Mon Dec 10, 2007 9:57 pm Shiro wrote: Just wait, when ELAM comes out (whenever that is) we get to say, "We knew about this first. And this sucks compared to THIS."

[]:             Quote Post by FinalImpactCrisis » Tue Dec 11, 2007 12:46 pm Matt_Colly wrote: Shiro wrote: Just wait, when ELAM comes out (whenever that is) we get to say, "We knew about this first. And this sucks compared to THIS."

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Dec 11, 2007 3:14 pm Shiro wrote: Hell yes. When Rebuild gets licensed, there'll be HUGE amounts of fans at cons, and ELAM will just make it bigger. Just wait, when ELAM comes out (whenever that is) we get to say, "We knew about this first. And this sucks compared to THIS." *stuff a Platinum thinpak in their hands* And Rebuild is going to be awesome. I can feel it. This is Anno's biggest project since, well, EoE, and he's already doing amazing. Webuild may end up being just as good or better than what we already have. And that will get people excited. Really excited.
